key_insight: This survey shows that many reinforcement learning algorithms can be understood and analyzed through shared mathematical tools such as Bellman operators, fixed-point theory, stochastic approximation, and convex optimization.
engineering_relevance: The framework helps engineers reason about RL stability, convergence, sample efficiency, and constraint handling when designing or deploying learning systems.
method: Model RL as MDPs and study Bellman operators via contraction and monotonicity.
Use stochastic approximation, martingale tools, and convex duality to analyze learning updates.
Extend the analysis to function approximation, off-policy learning, and constrained MDPs.
evidence_signal: The paper is a survey, so its evidence comes from theoretical convergence rates, finite-sample bounds, and asymptotic results rather than new experimental data.
watch_item: The guarantees can weaken under nonlinear function approximation, dependent data, or off-policy settings, so implementation details may strongly affect stability.
